Napoli have officially landed Matteo Politano from Inter on an 18-month loan with an obligation to buy.

President Aurelio De Laurentiis has confirmed Politano’s arrival on Twitter, after completing the negotiations with the Nerazzurri.

Politano comes on a €2.5m loan deal for the next 18 months and an obligation to buy the player for €19m plus €2m in bonuses linked with the club’s achievements during the next one and a half years.


Politano has been handed the No21 shirt at the San Paolo and will reportedly earn €2.2m plus a possible €300,000 in bonuses at Napoli.

quote.msg:
Napoli have officially landed Matteo Politano from Inter on an 18-month loan with an obligation to buy.

President Aurelio De Laurentiis has confirmed Politano’s arrival on Twitter, after completing the negotiations with the Nerazzurri.

Politano comes on a €2.5m loan deal for the next 18 months and an obligation to buy the player for €19m plus €2m in bonuses linked with the club’s achievements during the next one and a half years.


Politano has been handed the No21 shirt at the San Paolo and will reportedly earn €2.2m plus a possible €300,000 in bonuses at Napoli.

大簡受訪話想返黎教napoli

Guangzhou Evergrande coach Fabio Cannavaro 'dreams' of a return to Napoli and claims Milan 'made a mistake' by letting Gennaro Gattuso leave.

The former defender left Napoli after only three seasons with the first team at the San Paolo and he never returned as a player, but revealed he hopes to do so as a coach.

“I started coaching five years ago and I dream of one day coaching a great team, Napoli are one of them,” he told Sport Mediaset. “I have been lucky to have the best coaches of the last 50 years and I hope to take with me something from each of them.

“The day I decide to return to Europe, I hope I’ll be ready. I have always missed Naples, I left at 21 but remained very close.

“I have lived in many cities around the world, but I will end up in Naples, as a child I dreamed of becoming a symbol of the team.”


之前都ff過佢返黎教,但奶共奶到咁不了

As Poland star Arkadiusz Milik seems set to leave, Napoli are reportedly one step away from renewing with his compatriot Piotr Zielinski.

Il Mattino claims the two Poland internationals at the San Paolo are heading in opposite directions, with Zielinski close to agreeing new terms with the Partenopei.

Milik has been linked with a move away from the Azzurri and the striker has lately been targeted by both Milan in Serie A and Atletico Madrid in La Liga.


The newspaper writes about a phone call between Zielinski and the club, revealing the 25-year-old is close to signing a new deal and will supposedly do so as soon as Napoli resume play.

The contract shall have already been drafted, tying him down to another four years and Zielinski will reportedly go from €1.5m a year to a deal worth €2.5m annually, with a €100m release clause in his deal.

The attacking midfielder has settled well in Naples after arriving from Udinese in a €15.6m move in 2016 and has played 178 games for the Partenopei so far.

This term, he has appeared 26 times in Serie A, contributing two goals and three assists in the process.
